data.title: Bitcoin Breaks Above Mid-Term Holder Breakeven – Is A Fresh Rally Brewing?
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) has surged from around $108,000 on September 1 to above $115,000 at the time of writing – recording a gain of roughly 4% over the past two weeks. However, fresh on-chain data suggests that Bitcoin may be on the cusp of a fresh rally that could propel it to new all-time highs (ATH). Bitcoin Rises Above Mid-Term Holders’ Realized Price According to a CryptoQuant Quicktake post by contributor ShayanMarkets, Bitcoin’s recent rebound from $107,000 to just above $114,000 has lifted the digital asset over the Realized Price of mid-term (3-6 months) holders. Related Reading: Bitcoin Miners Shift Strategy: Accumulation Over Selling Signals Stronger Bull Cycle For the uninitiated, the mid-term holders’ Realized Price is the average acquisition cost of Bitcoin held by wallets that last moved their coins within the past 3–6 months
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/bitcoin-news/bitcoin-breaks-term-holder-breakeven-rally/

data.title: USDC circulation has decreased by approximately 100 million in the past 7 days
data.description: According to official data, in the seven days ending September 11th, Circle issued approximately 4.5 billion USDC and redeemed approximately 4.6 billion USDC, reducing the circulating supply by approximately 100 million. USDC now has a total circulating supply of 72.4 billion, with reserves of approximately $72.5 billion, including approximately $9.3 billion in cash and $63.2 billion held in the Circle Reserve Fund.

data.title: WORLD3 participates in Hash Global BNB dividend fund to establish long-term strategic reserves
data.description: WORLD 3 announced that it has become the first project to invest in Hash Global's compliant BNB Dividend Fund. This investment establishes a long-term strategic reserve and establishes a strategic partnership with Hash Global to jointly promote the development of the BNB ecosystem. Technically, WORLD 3 has deployed over 110,000 expert agents on the opBNB chain, completing over 2.8 million autonomous tasks, solidifying its position as a leading AI project since the launch of Binance Alpha. Capital-wise, Hash Global's BNB Dividend Fund plans to manage $100 million this year and expand to $3 billion within three years. Both parties stated that this partnership, combining AI-driven automation capabilities with compliant fund infrastructure, will provide strong support for WORLD 3's sustainable development within the BNB ecosystem by establishing a long-term strategic reserve and pioneering a new model for institutional participation in Web 3 AI.

data.title: El Salvador Increases Bitcoin Holdings by 28 Coins in Recent Week
data.description: According to PANews, El Salvador has increased its Bitcoin holdings by 28 coins over the past week. The country's total Bitcoin assets now amount to 6,317.18 coins, with an estimated value of $731 million.

data.title: Retail and institutional investors hold $7.6 trillion in cash, but won’t dump into Wall Street
data.description:
There’s now over $7. 6 trillion just sitting in money market funds, doing absolutely nothing flashy. That’s the highest on record, according to Crane Data, and most of this cash isn’t under mattresses or stuffed in sock drawers, but sitting in accounts earning around 4. 3% thanks to the Federal Reserve’s interest rate hikes. That yield has […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/retail-institutional-investors-7-6t-in-cash/

data.title: Hackers Stealing Funds from Coinbase Users Bought Nearly 4,000 ETH in the Last Half Hour
data.description: According to on-chain data analyst Ember, in the past half hour, hackers who stole funds from Coinbase users used 18.91 million DAI to purchase 3,976 ETH at an average price of $4,756.

data.title: Chainlink And Polymarket Forge New Alliance: LINK Forecasted To Outperform XRP By 2030
data.description:
Chainlink (LINK), one of the crypto market’s leading providers of decentralized oracle solutions, has announced a partnership with the prediction market platform Polymarket. Polymarket Integrates Chainlink On Polygon According to Friday’s announcement, the new integration is now live on the Polygon (POL) mainnet, enabling Polymarket to establish secure and real-time prediction markets centered around asset pricing, including numerous active cryptocurrency trading pairs. This collaboration also explores new methodologies to address more subjective questions. By doing so, Polymarket seeks to reduce its dependence on social voting mechanisms, thereby mitigating resolution risks in its markets
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/chainlink/chainlink-and-polymarket-forge-new-alliance-link-forecasted-to-outperform-xrp-by-2030/

data.title: Binance will adjust leverage and margin tiers for multiple U-margin perpetual contracts
data.description: According to an official announcement, Binance Futures will adjust the leverage and margin tiers for several U-margined perpetual swaps, including HIVEUSDT, POWERUSDT, SIRENUSDT, BNTUSDT, SQDUSDT, SKYAIUSDT, and MYXUSDT, starting at 06:30 (UTC) on September 15, 2025. The adjustments will be implemented in batches, impacting existing positions and requiring users to adjust their strategies in advance. (This newsletter was generated with AI assistance.)

data.title: Trump fraud allegation on Lisa Cook challenged as loan document sides with her
data.description:

The document, reported by Reuters, clearly shows Cook told her lender in May 2021 that the property was a “vacation home,” not her main residence. That puts her in direct conflict with claims […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-fraud-allegation-lisa-cook-challenged/

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs see $642M inflows as Fidelity and BlackRock lead
data.description:
13-9-2025 – Bitcoin spot exchange-traded funds posted a combined net inflow of $642 million on September 12, Eastern Time, according to data from SoSoValue. Fidelity’s FBTC fund dominated the session with $315 million in inflows, pushing its cumulative net inflows to $12. 627 billion. BlackRock’s IBIT followed with $265 million, elevating its historical total to $59
source: https://www.cointrackdaily.com/bitcoin-spot-etfs-see-642m-inflows-as-fidelity-and-blackrock-lead/

data.title: Fed unwind and US Treasury buildup drive up short-term borrowing costs
data.description:
Short-term borrowing is getting expensive again in the U. S. , and traders aren’t ignoring it. According to data from Bloomberg, the surge started when the Federal Reserve began slicing down its balance sheet while the Treasury ramped up cash collection. Both are yanking liquidity out of funding markets that’ve been flooded with cheap money for almost […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/fed-unwind-treasury-short-term-borrowing/

data.title: The current Crypto Fear and Greed Index is 53, which is neutral.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, the Crypto Fear and Greed Index is currently at 53, down 5 points from yesterday. The average over the past seven days is 50, and the average over the past 30 days is 51.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $642 million yesterday, marking the fifth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $642 million yesterday (September 12th, US Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow yesterday was the Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a net inflow of $315 million, bringing FBTC's total net inflow to $12.627 billion. The second largest single-day inflow was the Blackrock ETF IBIT, with a net inflow of $265 million, bringing IBIT's total net inflow to $59.778 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs was $153.178 billion, with a net asset value ratio (market capitalization relative to the total Bitcoin market capitalization) of 6.62%, and cumulative net inflows to $56.831 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $406 million yesterday, marking the fourth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $406 million yesterday (September 12th, EST). The Fidelity ETF (FETH) saw the largest single-day net inflow yesterday, with a net inflow of $168 million. FETH's total net inflow has now reached $2.859 billion. The second largest daily inflow was the Blackrock ETF (ETHA), with a net inflow of $166 million. ETHA's total net inflow has now reached $12.886 billion. As of press time, the Ethereum spot ETF's total net asset value (NAV) was $30.352 billion, with a 5.38% ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization). The cumulative net inflow has reached $13.363 billion.

data.title: Arthur Hayes: Bitcoin holders need more patience and don't need to worry about stocks and gold hitting new highs
data.description: In an interview with Kyle Chasse, BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes stated that Bitcoin holders need to be more patient and not anxious about the continuous record highs of stocks and gold. Questioning why Bitcoin hasn't broken through its previous highs completely misses the point. "If you think you can buy Bitcoin today and trade it in for a Lamborghini tomorrow, you're likely going to get wiped out—that mindset is inherently flawed. It's a shame you bought Bitcoin six months ago, but those who held on for two, three, five, or ten years are sitting on the sidelines. People need to recalibrate their perspective." Gold and the S&P 500 hit record highs of $3,674 and $6,587, respectively, this week, while Bitcoin remains below its all-time high of $124,100 reached on August 14th. Hayes downplayed the significance of these highs relative to Bitcoin and refuted questions about when Bitcoin and the crypto market would begin to attract global M2 inflows, arguing that the premise is flawed and that Bitcoin remains the best store of value when considering currency devaluation. Hayes noted that while the S&P 500 is up in dollar terms, it has yet to regain its 2008 levels when measured in gold. The real estate market is even worse when measured in gold terms, while US tech stocks are perhaps the only asset class still performing well when measured in gold terms. If other assets are measured in Bitcoin terms, the charts barely register—Bitcoin's outperformance is incredibly impressive.
